title: Secrets, GITHUB_TOKEN, Variables, Permissions, and Injection Defense · Delivery-System Trace: Ci Github Token Least Permission
summary: A workflow interpolates an issue title into shell, grants write-all globally, exposes a secret to an untrusted build, prints a derived credential, and mistakes masking for access control. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci github token least permission increment in workflow authority and data-flow policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Set default no or read authority and grant job-scoped GITHUB_TOKEN permissions only for exact API mutations.
Classify public configuration, non-secret variables, sensitive values, credentials, OIDC identity, and derived secrets by authority and lifetime.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0

  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment

content.title: Worked case pattern

Reduce permissions, separate data from code, rotate exposure, and retain redacted evidence. Competency probe: Set default no or read authority and grant job-scoped GITHUB_TOKEN permissions only for exact API mutations. A defensible solution applies Ci Github Token Least Permission, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to workflow authority and data-flow policy.

content.code:
# Worked focus: ci-github-token-least-permission — Set default no or read authority and grant job-scoped GITHUB_TOKEN permissions only for exact API mutations.
  # Evidence: ci-worked-gha-secrets-tokens-injection-2
  - competency: ci-worked-gha-secrets-tokens-injection-2
    artifact: authority-policy.yaml
    decision: worked gha secrets tokens injection 2
    environment: trusted release tag requiring an environment approval
    fault-injection: route five percent traffic to a revision with a data incompatibility
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked2
    change: |
      default-permissions: none
      checkout-job: contents-read
      attest-job: contents-read-id-token-write-attestations-write
      public-config: repository-variable
      secret: environment-scoped
      untrusted-input: validated-env-boundary
      shell: no-generated-code
      derived-secret: explicitly-masked
      rotation: rehearsed
    verify: trace every value from event and secret store through expression environment shell process log artifact cache and API mutation
    failure: Reject write-all defaults, expression-to-shell interpolation, masking as access control, transformed-secret auto-masking, or log deletion as response.
    repair: Classify data, grant per-job authority, validate untrusted input, prevent sinks, mask derived values, revoke, rotate, and test absence.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
